name: Braised Kosher-Cut Short Ribs
recipeIngredient: 1 Turnip, peeled and cut into 3-inch julienne pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large skillet. Add ribs and sauté for 2 minutes on each side or until browned. Remove from the pan and set aside.
2. Add another 2 tablespoons oil to the skillet and heat. Add cubed vegetables and bay leaf, and sauté for 5 minutes or until browned.
3. Place the sautéed vegetables and ribs in a roasting pan. Bake at 450°F for 15 to 20 minutes.
4. Add brandy and stock. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F and bake, covered, for 1 hour and 45 minutes to 2 hours, or until meat falls away from the bone.
5. Remove ribs from the pan and set aside. Discard cubed vegetables.
6. Strain the pan liquid.
7. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a small skillet. Stir in arrowroot until smooth. Cook and stir until golden brown.
8. Stir in half the strained liquid until well blended. Cook, stirring, until smooth and slightly thickened.
9. Gradually add the remaining strained liquid and continue cooking over medium-low heat, stirring until sauce is smooth and slightly thickened. Strain again.
10. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
11. Heat 1 to 2 tablespoons oil. Add julienne pieces of onion and turnip and sauté until lightly browned. Place the julienne vegetables on the short ribs and pour the sauce over all. Garnish with parsley.
